REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT 330 PRESIDIO AVENUE, LLC, JOE TOBONI DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED IN PART AND OVERRULED IN PART. JOE TOBONI'S DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND FOR PLAINTIFF TO ALLEGE FACTS GIVING RISE TO INDIVIDUAL LIABILITY UNDER EACH CAUSE OF ACTION OR FACTS SUPPORTING ALTER EGO. DEMURRER IS OTHERWISE OVERRULED. BASED ON THE INFORMATION AVAILABLE TO THE COURT AT THE DEMURRER STAGE THE COURT DOES NOT DETERMINE THAT COLLATERAL ESTOPPEL OR RES JUDICATA APPLIES. WHILE THE ISSUES RAISED IN THIS COMPLAINT WERE RAISED DURING ADMINISTRATIVE PROCEEDINGS, IT CANNOT BE ESTABLISHED AS A MATTER OF LAW THAT THEY HAVE IN FACT BEEN ADJUDICATED/CONSIDERED BY THE PERMITTING AGENCY OR THE BOARD OF APPEALS IN MAKING THE DETERMINATION REGARDING THE PERMIT. ADDITIONALLY, INDEPENDENT DUTY AND BREACH OF CONTRACT ARE SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGED. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: J. MURPHY; NOT REPORTED. (501)

PLAINTIFF'S EX PARTE APPLICATION TO CONTINUE TRIAL DATE HEARD ON 07/02/20 WAS DENIED BY THE COURT, HOWEVER ORAL ARGUMENT WOULD BE ENTERTAINED AT TRIAL CALL. THE COURT HEARD FURTHER ORAL ARGUMENT FROM BOTH SIDES AND DENIED THE MOTION TO CONTINUE THE TRIAL DATE. THE COURT ASSIGNED A SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE TO THE HONORABLE HAROLD KAHN IN DEPT. 505 FOR TODAY, 07/06/20, AT 1:30PM. THE COURT CONTINUED THE MATTER TO JUL-08-2020 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 206 FOR TRIAL ASSIGNMENT IF THE CASE DOES NOT SETTLE AT THE SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE. JUDGE: SAMUEL K. MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
James Michel, Esq., 415-239-4949, appearing for John Paxton, Plaintiff and moving party.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF JOHN PAXTON Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Third Amended Complaint is GRANTED. Non-opposition filed. Third Amended Complaint to be filed and served forthwith. Moving party and his counsel are ordered to comply with SFLR 2.7B in future. Failure to do so will result in motion being taken off calendar. Order for the same signed in open court. Judge Charles F. Haines, Clerk: N. Wong, Not reported (D501)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-08-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCE:
Justin Goodman, 415.956.8100 of Zacks, Freedman & Patterson for Defendants/Moving parties 330 Presidio Avenue, LLC et al.
James Michel, 415.239.4949 for Plaintiff/Opposing party for John Paxton.

Discussed. The Court rules as follows: DEFENDANT 330 PRESIDIO AVENUE, LLC, JOE TOBONI DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained in part and overruled in part. Joe Toboni's Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts giving rise to individual liability under each cause of action or facts supporting alter ego. Demurrer is otherwise overruled. Based on the information available to the Court at the demurrer stage the Court does not determine that collateral estoppel or res judicata applies. While the issues raised in this complaint were raised during administrative proceedings, it cannot be established as a matter of law that they have in fact been adjudicated/considered by the permitting agency or the Board of Appeals in making the determination regarding the permit. Additionally, independent duty and breach of contract are sufficiently alleged. Order signed. Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (501)

Order O Show Cause
Off Calendar Jan-09-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Justin Goodman, 415.956.8100 for Defendant 330 Presidio Avenue, LLC.
James Michel, 415.239.499 for Plaintiff John Paxon.

Argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: Order To Show Cause is DENIED. Plaintiff failed to carry its burden and show probability of prevailing on the merits and Defendants demonstrated that they are likely to suffer great injury from the granting of preliminary injunction. Defendant's Request for Judicial Notice is GRANTED. Order signed. Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (501)
